results.change_action: must equal mapped constant for Heartbeat demultiplexing
results.evidence_from_input: OCT == 24   -+--> Heartbeat (DTLS <1.3)
results.comment: Heartbeat appears in the RFC demux table, but BoringSSL has no Heartbeat feature or handler. Unsupported Heartbeat records are rejected/not dispatched, while processing Heartbeat records is unavailable.
results.comparison_summary: Requirement: when OCT == 24, Outer Content Type must must equal mapped constant for Heartbeat demultiplexing. Input evidence: OCT == 24   -+--> Heartbeat (DTLS <1.3). Code evidence: boringssl-main/ssl/dtls_record.cc:267; boringssl-main/ssl/d1_both.cc:419; boringssl-main/ssl/d1_pkt.cc:223. Heartbeat appears in the RFC demux table, but BoringSSL has no Heartbeat feature or handler. Unsupported Heartbeat records are rejected/not dispatched, while processing Heartbeat records is unavailable. Conclusion: partially satisfied.
results.standard_check: RFC 9147 Figure 5 maps OCT 24 and decrypted content type 24 to Heartbeat, but Heartbeat is an optional TLS/DTLS feature rather than mandatory DTLS 1.3 core behavior.
results.code_check: Static re-read found no SSL3_RT_HEARTBEAT or heartbeat handler. DTLS dispatch handles handshake, ACK, application data, alert, and CCS-specific paths.
results.test_check: focused_static_id152_153_185_187.py PASS and linked C harness PASS: heartbeat symbols and dispatch path are absent.
results.decision_reason: Unsupported Heartbeat is safely rejected/not dispatched, but the implementation cannot process Heartbeat records if that feature is required.

results.change_action: must equal mapped constant for encrypted_record with CID demultiplexing
results.evidence_from_input: OCT == 25   -+--> DTLSCiphertext with CID (DTLS 1.2)
results.comment: BoringSSL has no DTLS 1.2 tls12_cid demux path, rejects DTLS 1.3 records with the C bit set, and always sends DTLS 1.3 encrypted records with C=0.
results.comparison_summary: Requirement: when OCT == 25, Outer Content Type must must equal mapped constant for encrypted_record with CID demultiplexing. Input evidence: OCT == 25   -+--> DTLSCiphertext with CID (DTLS 1.2). Code evidence: boringssl-main/ssl/dtls_record.cc:170; boringssl-main/ssl/dtls_record.cc:431; boringssl-main/ssl/dtls_record.cc:533. BoringSSL has no DTLS 1.2 tls12_cid demux path, rejects DTLS 1.3 records with the C bit set, and always sends DTLS 1.3 encrypted records with C=0. Conclusion: not satisfied.
results.standard_check: RFC 9147 Section 4.1 includes OCT 25 for DTLSCiphertext with CID in DTLS 1.2, and DTLS 1.3 has an explicit CID bit in encrypted record headers.
results.code_check: parse_dtls13_record rejects the C bit, the sender fixes C=0, and no tls12_cid demux symbols are present.
results.test_check: focused_static_id152_153_185_187.py PASS and linked C harness PASS: CID symbols are absent, C-bit rejection exists, and the send header is fixed to C=0.
results.decision_reason: CID-capable operation is not implemented.

results.new_value: cid_spare

results.new_value: cid_immediate
